signals.signal_title: APA completes $4.5B Callon Petroleum acquisition — active Delaware Basin IT integration
signals.signal_description: APA Corporation completed its $4.5B acquisition of Callon Petroleum in April 2024, adding significant Delaware Basin scale. Post-merger IT integration of E&P systems (ERP, land, production data, SCADA) creates active near-term IT spend demand. Integration coincides with $350M run-rate cost savings program targeting technology-enabled efficiency through 2027.

enrichment_notes: FY2024 revenue $8.37B — independent E&P (Permian, Egypt, North Sea, Suriname). 4,700 employees. Houston HQ in STOLA — clean account. No CIO identified publicly. SAP probable. No MSP or CoE. Callon acquisition April 2024 = active IT integration demand. $350M cost savings program = technology efficiency signal. International operations (Egypt, North Sea, Suriname) require IT coordination. ai_maturity=7. Confidence LOW. WWT opportunity: Houston STOLA, no MSP, Callon integration demand — limited by low IT leadership visibility.
